meta.title: DubSync — AI Video Dubbing & Voice Cloning | Dub Videos in 30+ Languages
meta.description: Dub videos into 30+ languages with AI voice cloning and lip sync. Upload a video, get studio-quality dubbing in minutes. Free plan available.

whatIs.title: What is AI video dubbing?
whatIs.p1: AI video dubbing is the process of automatically translating and re-recording a video's audio in another language using artificial intelligence. Unlike traditional dubbing that requires voice actors and studio time, AI dubbing uses voice cloning technology to replicate the original speaker's voice in the target language — preserving their tone, emotion, and speaking style.
whatIs.p2: DubSync combines three AI technologies: neural machine translation for accurate script conversion, voice cloning to maintain speaker identity across languages, and lip synchronization to match mouth movements with the new audio. The result is a dubbed video that looks and sounds natural in any language.

howItWorks.title: How DubSync works
howItWorks.subtitle: DubSync uses AI to automate the entire video dubbing workflow — from transcription to voice cloning to lip sync — in three simple steps.
howItWorks.step1desc: Upload any video file (MP4, MOV, AVI up to 5GB). DubSync automatically transcribes the original audio and detects the source language.
howItWorks.step2desc: Our AI translates the transcript, clones the speaker's voice in the target language, and generates lip-synced audio that matches mouth movements. The entire process takes 2–5 minutes for a typical 10-minute video.
howItWorks.step3desc: Review the result, make any edits to the translation, and download your dubbed video in up to 4K quality. Share it with your global audience instantly.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.a1: DubSync uses AI to analyze the speaker's voice characteristics — pitch, tone, accent, and emotion — from the original video. It then generates new speech in the target language that preserves these characteristics.
faq.a2: DubSync supports MP4, MOV, AVI, WebM, and MKV formats. The maximum file size depends on your plan.
faq.a3: Most videos are processed in 2-5 minutes. A typical 10-minute video takes about 3 minutes to dub into one language.
faq.a4: Yes. The free plan includes 1 video up to 1 minute with lip sync and voice cloning. No credit card required.
faq.q5: How accurate is the lip sync?
faq.a5: DubSync uses AI lip-sync technology to automatically adjust mouth movements to match the new audio, with 95-98% accuracy.
faq.q6: Can I edit the translation before dubbing?
faq.a6: Yes. After the AI generates the translation, you can review and edit the script before generating the final dubbed audio.
faq.a7: DubSync supports over 30 languages including Spanish, French, German, Japanese, Korean, Chinese, Hindi, Arabic, Portuguese, and more.
faq.a8: Yes. DubSync automatically detects and separates multiple speakers, cloning each voice individually.
faq.q9: How much does AI video dubbing cost?
faq.a9: DubSync offers a free plan (1 video up to 1 minute), Starter at $19.99/month (20 credits), Pro at $49.99/month (50 credits), and Business at $149.99/month (150 credits). Annual billing saves 20%. Every credit includes lip sync.
faq.q10: What does 1 credit mean in DubSync?
faq.a10: 1 credit = 1 minute of dubbed video in 1 target language, always with lip sync included. A 5-minute video into 3 languages uses 15 credits.
faq.q11: Does DubSync include lip sync on all plans?
faq.a11: Yes. Every paid plan includes lip sync in every credit. Even the free plan includes lip sync.
faq.q12: Is DubSync better than traditional dubbing?
faq.a12: For digital content, marketing videos, e-learning, and social media — yes. AI dubbing is 10-100x faster and more affordable.
faq.a13: Yes. AI subtitles synced to dubbed audio. Burned-in for social media or SRT/VTT for YouTube. All plans.
faq.q14: Can I export SRT subtitle files?
faq.a14: Yes. SRT or VTT from any dubbed video. Upload to YouTube, use in LMS, or edit externally.
